summary: Soundboard capture of a mid‑71 Dead show with the band firing on all cylinders; reviewers love the high‑energy vibe and the hilarious “Okie From Muscogee,” though one critic balks at the NRPS set.
review: The recording (a soundboard source) is generally clean, with no audible flaws mentioned. Musically the concert shines – a “mid‑71 bar band on steroids” that delivers loose‑but‑lovable jams and a standout performance of the tongue‑in‑cheek “Okie From Muscogee.” The only sour note is a reviewer’s dislike of the NRPS set and a gripe about Pig Pen’s organ part being swapped for Merl Saunders on an album version.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 4‑star review praises the show and notes good soundboard quality; a second 0‑star comment is narrowly focused on the NRPS set, giving an overall mixed but leaning‑positive impression.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio complaints; reviewers assume a clear soundboard capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert maintains a high‑energy, mid‑71 vibe with occasional looseness; overall the performance outweighs any sloppiness.

band_member_comments.Keys: Reviewer notes that Pig Pen’s organ on “Bertha” was replaced by Merl Saunders on the Skullfuck album and prefers Pig’s more rollicking playing.
